summary: 6 newsletters ingested. High-signal batch: GLM-5.2 open agents analysis (critical for wiki), Anthropic government policy shift (critical event), AI agent loops in Claude Code/Codex (high relevance). SpaceX neocloud and red-teaming after Mythos also notable.

classifications.subject: Import AI 462: Superpersuasion; self-sustaining AI; paths to ASI
classifications.rationale: Jack Clark's Import AI covers three AI safety-adjacent topics: superpersuasion (AI's ability to persuade humans at scale), self-sustaining AI systems, and pathways to ASI. Directly relevant to wiki concepts on AI safety and AI risk. The newsletter has only 16 links (mostly Substack boilerplate), so the actual content depth is limited in this digest.

classifications.subject: GLM-5.2 is the step change for open agents
classifications.rationale: GLM-5.2 has been on a tear in recent AINews (June 17, 19 already covered its coding benchmarks and vibe check). This follow-up positions it specifically as a step change for open agent use cases. Directly impacts wiki entities (Zhipu/GLM) and concepts (open-source LLMs, coding agents). Multiple prior raw newsletters already exist covering GLM-5.2 benchmarks.

classifications.subject: How I AI: How to write AI agent loops in Claude Code and Codex + How Claude Mythos found a 15-year-old bug
classifications.rationale: Practical guide on writing agent loops in Claude Code and Codex — directly relevant to wiki's coding agents coverage (claude-code, codex skills exist). Also covers Claude Mythos finding a 15-year-old bug, which demonstrates advanced AI debugging capability. High signal for agent coding workflows.

classifications.subject: Washington Pulls the Plug on Anthropic
classifications.source: Beehiiv newsletter (unknown publisher, likely The Neuron or similar)
classifications.rationale: Major policy event: Washington pulling support/contracts from Anthropic. This could signal significant shifts in AI safety policy and government AI procurement. Anthropic is a core wiki entity. Beehiiv URLs are tracking redirects (not standard article URLs) — content needs to be fetched directly from the newsletter publisher.

classifications.subject: Red-Teaming after Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
classifications.rationale: Latent Space podcast episode on red-teaming post-Mythos with Zico Kolter (CMU, adversarial ML) and Matt Fredrikson. Gray Swan is a safety-focused entity. The discussion of red-teaming after the Mythos incident is highly relevant to AI safety evaluation and adversarial robustness concepts in the wiki.

classifications.subject: [AINews] SpaceX is already a $28B/yr Neocloud
classifications.rationale: SpaceX as a $28B/yr neocloud is a significant AI infrastructure story — covers the GPU cloud compute economics that are central to AI training/inference. Relevant to wiki concepts around AI infrastructure and compute. Also mentions Clouded Judgement (VC/finance analysis).

notes: All 6 newsletters contain only Substack/beehiiv redirect URLs in the raw digest — no article body content was extracted. The newsletter-wiki-ingest pipeline needs to follow the content_url to fetch actual article text.
Beehiiv newsletter (#4) uses opaque tracking URLs that may not resolve without proper referrer headers — may need alternative approach.
GLM-5.2 is building as a recurring topic across multiple newsletters (June 17, 19, 22) — wiki entity page update is warranted.
Anthropic policy news (#4) is likely time-sensitive and should be prioritized for wiki event page creation.
